verifyCommand: CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o /dev/null ./cmd/certstrike && CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...
acceptanceCriteria: Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
All tests pass
MS-ICPR RPC enrollment works via ICertPassage SMB pipe
UPN SAN passed via CertAttrib in web enrollment
RC promote-to-GitHub checklist complete

features.title: Verify MS-ICPR RPC enrollment via ICertPassage over SMB named pipe
acceptanceCriteria: ICertPassage interface or struct exists in pkg/pki/
SMB named pipe connection to cert service is implemented
RPC enrollment path calls ICertPassage RequestCertificate (or equivalent)
CLI flag or auto-detection selects RPC vs web enrollment
Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
Tests covering RPC enrollment path exist and pass
features.notes: Committed in 5dac0a7 - verified: ICertPassage struct/UUID in rpc_enroll.go, SMB named pipe to \pipe\cert, CertServerRequest opnum 0, auto-detection (RPC first, fallback to web), tests added in rpc_enroll_test.go

features.title: Verify UPN SAN via CertAttrib in web enrollment
acceptanceCriteria: Web enrollment (certsrv) passes UPN SAN via certificate attributes, not CSR
CertAttrib field includes san:upn= or equivalent attribute
ESC6 exploitation still works with SAN injection via request attributes
No regression in ESC1 enrollment (enrollee-supplied subject)
Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
Tests pass
features.notes: Verified: sanInject=true omits UPN from CSR (enroll.go:79-80), SAN:upn= built at enroll.go:146 and appended to CertAttrib at enroll.go:216-218, ESC6 calls with sanInject=true (esc6.go:113), ESC7 calls with sanInject=true (esc7.go:233), ESC1 calls with sanInject=false (adcs.go:409) preserving enrollee-supplied subject. Build + tests pass.

features.title: Verify no misleading per-ESC next steps with wrong file paths
acceptanceCriteria: No ESC exploit output references nonexistent file paths
Next steps commands reference correct output filenames (cert.pem, key.pem, cert.pfx)
All ESC exploit functions print consistent follow-up commands
Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
features.notes: Verified: exploit ESCs (1-4,6,7,9,13) share common handler writing .crt/.key/.pfx and referencing those exact extensions in output (pki.go:800). Scan-only ESCs (5,8,10,11,12,14) print appropriate next-step commands without referencing nonexistent files. PKINIT commands use actual PFXPath from handler. No .pem references in user-facing output. Build + tests pass.

features.title: Fix certipy-ad cert PEM-to-PFX conversion command syntax
acceptanceCriteria: pkinit.go PEM-to-PFX conversion uses correct certipy-ad syntax: 'certipy-ad cert -export -pfx <out.pfx> -cert <cert.pem> -key <key.pem>' (not 'certipy-ad cert -pfx -cert ... -key ... -out ...')
Alternatively use openssl pkcs12 syntax if certipy-ad cert subcommand is unreliable
Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
Tests pass
features.notes: pkinit.go:42 has wrong certipy-ad cert syntax. Real certipy-ad uses: certipy-ad cert -pfx <file> -export or openssl pkcs12 -export -in cert.pem -inkey key.pem -out cert.pfx

features.title: Fix Rubeus asktgt /user parameter to use sAMAccountName not full UPN
acceptanceCriteria: unpac.go Rubeus.exe asktgt /user: uses stripped username (no @domain), not full UPN
pkinit.go Rubeus.exe asktgt /user: uses stripped username (no @domain)
All Rubeus commands use sAMAccountName format for /user: parameter
Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
Tests pass
features.notes: unpac.go:25 passes full UPN to Rubeus /user: but Rubeus expects sAMAccountName. Line uses 'upn' var instead of 'user' var.

features.title: Fix ntlmrelayx ESC11/ESC12 chain.go commands - CA name vs template name
acceptanceCriteria: chain.go ESC11 ntlmrelayx -icpr-ca-name uses CA name variable (not template name)
chain.go ESC12 ntlmrelayx command uses valid flags for DCOM relay
autopwn.go ESC11 relay command (line ~261) uses CA name not template name for -icpr-ca-name
Build passes with CGO_ENABLED=0
Tests pass
features.notes: chain.go:188 passes tmpl.Name to -icpr-ca-name but this should be the CA name. chain.go:196 uses -dcom-mode which may not be a real ntlmrelayx flag.

acceptanceCriteria: CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o /dev/null ./cmd/certstrike succeeds
CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... succeeds (all packages)
No compilation warnings or errors
go vet ./... passes clean
